ead.archdesc:
======= RMM07562David Patrick McIntosh papers7562Compiled by Catherine TingeyDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
July 19942B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
humsx dramaThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 19:02:19 UTC.12/07/2018

converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
David Patrick McIntosh papers
McIntosh, David, 1950-1989.
7562
3.5 cubic feet. 3.5 cubic feet.
1965-1989. Audio tapes, correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, plays. Correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, and plays by David McIntosh. Includes audio tapes of his short opera, Can. Collection material in English <<<<<<< HEAD 8 9 ======= 8 9 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab 2098479
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
C. Tingey
Date completed:
July 1994
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, April 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
David Patrick McIntosh was born in Philadelphia and grew up in Omaha, Nebraska, where he attended a Jesuit school. He later moved to Tennessee, where worked with an alternative theater company in Knoxville, The Play Group, from 1974-1979, and then moved to New York City. In New York he wrote and worked both in the theatre and at the New York City Gay and Lesbian Center where he designed an ornamental garden. He died from complications of AIDS in 1989.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence, journals, poetry, songs, fiction, and plays by David McIntosh. Includes audio tapes of his short opera, Can.
RELATED MATERIALS
Collecting program: Human Sexuality Collection.
Cite As:
David Patrick McIntosh papers, #7562. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Restrictions on Use:
Charles Dean retains literary rights until January 1, 2017. Until that time, people may view the collection, but may not publish any individual's name (other than David McIntosh's) seen in the collection without Dean's consent or permission from that individual. Researchers must sign an agreement to this effect.
Dramatists. Gay liberation movement -- New York (State) -- New York. Gardens -- Design. Experimental theater -- New York (State) -- New York. Gay men\'s writings. Gay men. Play Group (Knoxville, Tenn.) New York City Gay and Lesbian Center
